videos.title: Everything you need to know about leeches (they suck) with Tim Cockerill #shorts #science #leeches
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description:
Would you let a leech feed on your arm for science? 🩸 Entomologist Tim Cockerill did just that.⁠
⁠
He knew leeches have around 300 teeth...and still did it. If that isn't commitment to science!

videos.title: Liquid oxygen has paramagnetic properties 🧲⁠ #shorts #scienceexperiment #science #educationalvideo
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description:
When you pour it between two poles of a strong magnet (at room temperature) the liquid is held there until it boils back into a gas.

videos.title: How nanoparticles are quietly revolutionising the world | with Ivan Parkin
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description:
What if the glass in your conservatory was quietly cleaning itself right now? It probably is — and it's all down to a coating just 25 nanometres thick.

The talk was livestreamed and filmed at the Ri on 24th April 2026, in celebration of UCL's 200th anniversary.

videos.title: The mathematical modelling of jellyfish with Becky Shipley #shorts #sciencefacts #jellyfish #biology
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description:
As jellyfish move, they generate vortices in patterns that also appear in the human heart. 🪼
Healthcare Engineer Becky Shipley illustrates how simple models can lead to life-changing insights. Full talk on our channel and linked above!
